location.office_time_required: no mandatory office time; Flex First policy with regular in-person events

level_signals: 4+ years of product management experience minimum
Base pay range $184K–$233K depending on state, consistent with Senior PM at a tech company
Owns vision, strategy, and execution for a product area
Interfaces with senior stakeholders and external partners

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of product management experience, ideally within operationally complex physical environments
must_haves.qualifier: ideally within operationally complex physical environments

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You've built products end-to-end and know how to drive initiatives from early concept through scaled delivery.
You're highly data-literate and comfortable navigating ambiguity with analytical rigor, using data to prioritize ruthlessly and make sound tradeoffs.
You communicate complex ideas with clarity and influence — aligning cross-functional teams and senior stakeholders around shared goals without direct authority.
You understand the shopper and retailer experience deeply, and you hold yourself accountable to meaningful customer and commercial outcomes.
You operate with an entrepreneurial mindset, comfortable moving fast in evolving environments while maintaining high standards.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4+ years of product management experience, ideally within operationally complex physical environments

scope.surface: In-Store Tasks product — shopper in-store data collection experiences powering inventory intelligence and store maps
scope.business_model: marketplace / platform (B2B2C: serving retailers, CPGs, shoppers, and end customers)
core_responsibilities: Own the vision, strategy, and execution for the In Store Tasks product
Define and deliver the roadmap for shoppers doing in-store tasks to build in-store intelligence
Own data collection experiences from shopper task receipt through inventory and store map data collection
Build deep understanding of shopper workflows and operational pain points to design tools improving scanning accuracy, speed, and satisfaction
Work closely with retail partners to ensure tooling integrates into in-store environments
Partner with enterprise sales and retailer success teams to support partner onboarding and drive feature adoption
Define platform solutions that scale across retailer sizes and store formats
Represent the in-store task roadmap with internal stakeholders and external partners

compensation.equity_form: new hire equity grant plus annual refresh grants

green_flags: Compensation range fully disclosed across all US geographies
Equity grant (new hire + annual refresh) explicitly mentioned
Fully remote role (Flex First policy) with no mandatory office percentage
Clear product surface and team context (Inventory Intelligence team)
High-impact, foundational product area with explicit 2-year scale ambition
Cross-functional scope clearly described (engineering, design, data science, sales, retailers)
red_flags: Work authorization and visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
'Ideally within operationally complex physical environments' is listed under Minimum Qualifications but uses hedging language — classification as nice-to-have is an inference
No mention of reporting structure or career ladder context

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and scope of ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from product and customer description)
compensation.base_range_usd min/max (taken as lowest-state floor and highest-state ceiling across all bands)
ambiguities: 'ideally within operationally complex physical environments' appears as a qualifier on the 4+ years minimum qualification bullet — it could be a soft preference or a strong preference. Treated as nice-to-have (soft) due to the word 'ideally', but also captured in the must-have for completeness with a qualifier field.
The JD is tagged #LI-Remote and describes Flex First policy, but no explicit statement is made about US-only remote eligibility. Treated as remote-us given pay bands are US-state-specific.
